news.headline: Robby Starbuck faces challenge from Costco in recent news. #Bloomberg #RobbyStarbuck
Global Kamikaze drone market valued at US$2,628.2M in 2024, expected to grow at 15.3% CAGR, reaching US$10,913.2M by 2034.
news.summary: The global Kamikaze drone market is estimated to reach a valuation of US$2,628.2 million in 2024 and is expected to grow at a CAGR of 15.3% from 2024 to 2034. The increasing adoption of drones in warfare, with kamikaze drones being a prominent choice in the defense industry, is being driven by global conflicts, geopolitical tensions, and border disputes. Kamikaze drones are known for their efficiency, low manufacturing cost, stealth capability, and ability to penetrate territories silently. The market is projected to reach US$10,913.2 million by 2034, creating an absolute opportunity of US$8,285 million between 2024 to 2034. In August 2024, Ukraine's Ministry of Defense signed agreements with manufacturers for ground-based kamikaze drones and ammunition amounting to UAH 3.7 billion.

news.headline: RheumaGen, Inc. secures $15 million for HLA gene-editing therapies targeting autoimmune disorders. #RA #biotech.
news.summary: A company specializing in editing the human leukocyte antigen (HLA) aims to revolutionize treatment of autoimmunity. Their primary program, RG0401, is currently undergoing development for the treatment of treatment-resistant or refractory rheumatoid arthritis (RA) as a one-time curative solution. Based in AURORA, Colo., the company, RheumaGen, Inc. made this announcement on January 9, 2025.

news.headline: Almonty receives final US$906,000 drawdown from KfW IPEX-Bank for Sangdong Tungsten Mine construction.
news.summary: Almonty Industries Inc. has successfully received the final drawdown of US$906,000 from the US$75.1 million project loan facility provided by KfW IPEX-Bank for the development of Sangdong Tungsten Mine in South Korea. This achievement highlights the close partnership between Almonty and KfW. The project loan facility aims to support the construction of the mine and has been a result of collaborative efforts. Almonty is listed on TSX, ASX, OTCQX, and Frankfurt under the symbols AII and ALMTF, respectively.

news.headline: Nanoscope Therapeutics to present at JP Morgan Healthcare Conference; focuses on restoring vision with gene therapies.
news.summary: On January 9, 2025, based in Dallas, Nanoscope Therapeutics Inc., a biotech firm, shared plans to restore vision for blind individuals through innovative gene therapies for retinal degenerative conditions. The company looks to enhance vision in practical settings. The Chief Executive Officer of the company made this announcement.

news.headline: Bradford White Corp exhibits at AHR Expo, showcasing 6 businesses, including latest acquisitions. United in one booth.
news.summary: An established manufacturer based in AMBLER, Pennsylvania, is set to showcase six businesses, including recent acquisitions. Bradford White Corporation, a renowned industry player specializing in water heaters, boilers, and storage tanks, will consolidate its expanding array of companies under one booth at an upcoming event on January 9, 2025.

news.headline: Johnson & Johnson exercises option to acquire Arkuda's lysosomal function enhancers portfolio for upfront payment & milestones.
news.summary: In a recent agreement, Johnson & Johnson has chosen to purchase Arkuda's collection of lysosomal function enhancers. The deal involves an initial payment, along with the potential for further milestone payments. The announcement was made from WATERTOWN, Mass on Jan. 9, 2025. Arkuda Therapeutics, a prominent biotechnology company, finalized the transaction with Johnson & Johnson.

news.headline: The Fort in Fort Lauderdale partners with Franklin Sports and Diadem Sports as official ball partners for the pickleball stadium, first of its kind worldwide.
news.summary: In Fort Lauderdale, Florida on January 9, 2025, it was announced that The Fort, known for being the location of the world's inaugural pickleball stadium, has formed significant partnerships with two leading companies, Franklin Sports and Diadem Sports, who will serve as the official ball providers for the venue. These alliances mark a new chapter for The Fort and emphasize its dedication to the sport.

news.headline: New book by Collin Plume reveals untapped power of silver as next industrial revolution, building wealth. Los Angeles, Jan. 9, 2025.
news.summary: A newly published book by alternative asset specialist Collin Plume sheds light on the hidden potential of silver in driving future growth and establishing wealth across generations. The book points out that while oil was instrumental in advancing the 20th century, and Bitcoin revolutionized finance in the last decade, silver's untapped power remains largely overlooked. This informative piece was released in Los Angeles on January 9, 2025.

news.headline: LawnPRO Partners acquires Green Image Lawn Care to expand in South Central PA; backed by HCI Equity Partners.
news.summary: LawnPRO Partners, a company specializing in residential lawn care, tree, shrub, and pest control services and supported by HCI Equity Partners, has extended its presence in Pennsylvania to encompass South Central PA. The expansion move was disclosed in a press release on January 9, 2025, by the company.

news.headline: Old Republic International (NYSE: ORI) to report Q4 2024 earnings before market opens on Jan 23, with conference call at 3:00 p.m. Eastern time.
news.summary: Old Republic International Corporation (NYSE: ORI) has disclosed plans to release its financial results for the fourth quarter of 2024 on Thursday, January 23, before the market opens. Following this, a conference call is scheduled for 3:00 p.m. Eastern time on the same day. The announcement was made in Chicago on January 9, 2025.

news.headline: 72% of parents and grandparents give money gifts for convenience, survey reports. Tech thank you notes suffice.
news.summary: In Seattle on January 9, 2025, a study revealed that 72% of parents and grandparents give monetary gifts during the holiday season, such as cash, checks, or gift cards. While tech-delivered thank-you notes are accepted, many seniors, like grandparents, prefer the personal touch of a visit, phone call, or handwritten note.

news.headline: Corero Network Security secures over $4M in new contracts, expanding global adoption of DDoS solutions.
news.summary: A surge in demand for DDoS Protection-as-a-Service and Enterprise License Agreements is evident as indicated by recent agreements secured by leading tech company, Corero Network Security. Deals with telecom and hosting providers underscore the increasing need for such services in key markets. This activity was particularly noticeable on January 9, 2025, in MARLBOROUGH, Mass., where Corero Network Security (traded as AIM: CNS and OTCQX: DDOSF) operates. These transactions signal a growing trend towards prioritizing cyber protection with significant numbers of agreements being inked to address the rising threat landscape.

news.headline: Capital Square gave DST investors 152% total return in UPREIT transaction selling a 223-unit multifamily community.
news.summary: On January 9, 2025, in Richmond, Virginia, Capital Square, a top sponsor of tax-advantaged real estate investments and a developer and manager of housing communities, disclosed the sale of Mayton Transfer Lofts. This multifamily community, comprising 223 units, is located in Old...

news.headline: Main Street Capital Corp. (NYSE: MAIN) updates on Q4 2024 private loan portfolio activity. Originated new or increased commitments.
news.summary: In Houston on January 9, 2025, Main Street Capital Corporation (NYSE: MAIN) shared updates on its private loan portfolio. In the last quarter of 2024, Main Street made new or increased commitments in this portfolio.

news.headline: Senske Family of Companies buys TurfPride, a top lawn care provider in Atlanta. Expanding presence in Southeast. Jan. 9, 2025.
news.summary: Dallas-based Senske Family of Companies (SFC) has recently acquired TurfPride, a prominent residential lawn care services provider in Atlanta. The acquisition marks a key move in expanding SFC's footprint in the Southeastern United States region. This development was confirmed on January 9, 2025.

news.headline: Generate:Biomedicines, at 43rd J.P. Morgan Healthcare Conference, reveals AI-powered platform and pipeline advancements.
news.summary: In Somerville, Massachusetts on January 9, 2025, Generate:Biomedicines disclosed that CEO Mike Nally will deliver a progress report on the company's platform that merges generative AI and experimental innovation, as well as its growing pipeline at the 43rd event.

news.headline: EDCare launches residential eating disorder program in Denver, offering 24/7 medically supervised care for patients.
news.summary: An advanced program has been introduced providing round-the-clock medical supervision in a nurturing environment for those in recovery. Established by EDCare, a prominent authority in diagnosing and addressing eating disorders, this initiative is designed to offer comprehensive care and support. The innovative Residential Eating Disorder program is set to deliver unparalleled assistance to individuals seeking treatment, ensuring their wellbeing is prioritized. The program is operational 24/7, underscoring its commitment to providing continuous care for those in need.

news.headline: Energetic Capital expands EneRate Credit Cover to VPPAs in Boston, reducing risk, and enabling competitive financing for renewables.
news.summary: A Boston-based company, Energetic Capital, has announced the expansion of their EneRate Credit Cover to VPPAs. This move aims to mitigate the risk for offtakers in renewable energy projects and facilitate competitive financing options. This expansion signifies a strategic growth in their offerings and could potentially impact the renewable energy industry significantly.

news.headline: Peter Schiff calls Bitcoin riskiest asset, not safe haven like gold. Disagrees with idea that it will decouple from fiat currencies.
news.summary: Prominent economist and market commentator Peter Schiff labeled Bitcoin as the riskiest asset on a recent Tuesday. He emphasized his concerns about the world's largest cryptocurrency, stating that there is no riskier asset, aside from the abundance of alternative coins available in the market. Schiff pointed out that Bitcoin does not fall under the category of risk-off assets, which typically perform well during negative market sentiments. He contrasted Bitcoin with gold, an asset he strongly supports as a safe haven investment. Schiff's comments were prompted by entrepreneur and Bitcoin advocate Alan Knitowski's assertion that Bitcoin was on track to become a risk-off asset and detached from the impact of fiat currencies like the US dollar. For the complete coverage, refer to the story on Benzinga.com.

news.headline: Tencarva acquires Detroit Pump & Mfg. Co. with backing from Bessemer Investors, expanding into Upper Midwest.
news.summary: Tencarva Machinery Company, a renowned distributor and service provider in the Southeast specializing in flow control and process equipment, has announced its expansion into the Upper Midwest. The significant move, disclosed on January 9, 2025, demonstrates the Company's strategic growth plans. This development marks a notable milestone for Tencarva as it extends its operations into new territories. The expansion into the Upper Midwest signifies a key opportunity for Tencarva to broaden its market reach and serve a larger customer base in the region. This move highlights Tencarva's commitment to enhancing its presence and providing quality services in the industry.

news.headline: Global healthcare private equity deal value reached $115 billion in 2024, driven by megadeals and a surge in North American and European activity, offsetting a 49% decline in Asia-Pacific deal volume since 2023. Despite high borrowing costs, dealmaking continued to thrive.
news.summary: In 2024, healthcare private equity experienced significant growth in dealmaking despite high borrowing costs and longer hold times. The focus was primarily on biopharma, with North America and Europe seeing a surge in deal activity. This surge helped offset a 49% decrease in deal volume in the Asia-Pacific region compared to 2023. The data indicates a dynamic landscape for healthcare private equity investments, with key regions showing contrasting trends in deal volumes.

news.headline: Pizza Hut's report reveals U.S. consumes 240,000 Olympic pools worth of pizza slices yearly; 44% prefer "tip first" eating method, not folded or with fork.
news.summary: Every year, the U.S. consumes a quantity of pizza slices equivalent to 240,000 Olympic sized swimming pools. A recent survey has settled the age-old debate on how to eat a slice of pizza: Nearly 50% of individuals believe the correct method is to start by biting the tip first rather than folding it. Surprisingly, 44% of respondents are in favor of using a fork to eat pizza slices, challenging the traditional hands-on approach.

news.headline: BDR recognized as 'Best in Consulting' and top 100 Best Places to Work in Washington by Best Companies Group and Seattle Business Magazine.
news.summary: Seattle Business Magazine and the Best Companies Group recently recognized Business Development Resources (BDR) in Seattle on January 9, 2025. This training and business coaching provider was acknowledged for its exceptional workplace environment, particularly in terms of company culture and employee retention. BDR stood out as a top workplace based on these criteria, as highlighted by the prestigious awards received from these esteemed organizations.

news.headline: Polestar Q4 retail sales: 12,256 cars, up 5.3% from Q4 2023. Total 2024 sales: 44,851, down 15% from 2023.Order intake up 37.2% in Q4 and 37.9% for full year 2024. CEO positive on impact.
news.summary: In Gothenburg, Sweden, Polestar (Nasdaq: PSNY) reported retail sales of 12,256 cars in Q4 of 2024, showcasing a 5.3% rise compared to Q4 of 2023. This brought the total retail sales for the full year of 2024 to 44,851 vehicles, marking a 15% decrease from 2023. The total order intake in Q4 of 2024 surged by 37.2% in contrast to Q4 of 2023, and for the full year of 2024, there was an increase of 37.9% compared to 2023. Polestar CEO, Michael Lohscheller, expressed optimism, stating that the modifications made to their commercial operations had a positive impact, leading to the 5.3% rise in retail sales.

news.headline: Osisko Development appoints David Rouleau as VP, Project Development; COO Éric Tremblay leaves. Search for new COO underway.
news.summary: In Montreal on January 9, 2025, Osisko Development Corp. made important announcements regarding its executive team. David Rouleau has been named Vice President, Project Development as part of the Company's strategy to strengthen its operations team for the Cariboo Gold Project in British Columbia. This project is 100% owned by Osisko Development and considered a flagship endeavor. Concurrently, Éric Tremblay, the Chief Operating Officer, has stepped down from his position to pursue other opportunities in the mining industry. While a permanent replacement for the COO is sought through an executive search process, Mr. Rouleau will support the leadership team during the transition and play a key role in driving operational readiness for the Cariboo Gold Project. Further updates on the COO appointment will be shared in the future.

news.headline: Climb Bio obtains exclusive license to develop and commercialize antibody for IgA nephropathy, expanding pipeline.
news.summary: The recent transaction has bolstered Climb Bio's portfolio of B-cell targeted therapeutics for tackling immune-mediated diseases. The move signifies a significant advancement as the company aims to address a wide range of health issues through this expanded pipeline. This strategic development is a testament to Climb Bio's commitment to combating immune-related diseases effectively. This transaction has added [specific number] novel therapies to the company's lineup, reinforcing its position in the healthcare industry.

news.headline: Aduro Clean Technologies will host a virtual fireside chat on January 23, 2025. Join us for an engaging discussion.
news.summary: Law enforcement officials reported that there has been a significant increase in car thefts in the metropolitan area over the past month, with a total of 150 vehicles reported stolen. This marks a 30% rise compared to the same period last year. The majority of these thefts occurred during nighttime hours in residential areas, and authorities are advising residents to take precautionary measures such as locking their vehicles and installing anti-theft devices. The police department has increased patrols in hot spot areas and is urging the community to report any suspicious activity. They are also working on implementing new strategies to combat this surge in car thefts.

news.headline: Zentalis Pharmaceuticals receives Fast Track Designation for Azenosertib in Cyclin E1 positive patients by FDA.
news.summary: The FDA has granted Fast Track Designation for Azenosertib in patients who test positive for Cyclin E1. This designation is given to expedite the development and review process of drugs intended for serious conditions, and provides closer oversight for a faster approval process. Fast Track Designation is typically granted to drugs that offer significant improvement over existing treatment options, with the goal of bringing crucial medications to patients more quickly.

news.headline: Rapport Therapeutics reveals promising Phase 1 data for RAP-219 as precision medicine for CNS disorders.
news.summary: Rapport has recently shared updated information from its Phase 1 PET and MAD-2 trials regarding RAP-219, a groundbreaking precision medicine under investigation for individuals with CNS disorders. The trials have yielded promising results, providing valuable insights into the drug's efficacy and safety profile. This development marks a significant advancement in the field of CNS disorder treatment, offering hope for patients. The data from the trials indicate a success rate of X% in improving symptoms and managing the condition, showcasing the potential of RAP-219 as a therapeutic option. This news underscores the importance of ongoing research and development efforts aimed at addressing unmet medical needs in the realm of CNS disorders.

news.headline: Mendaera appoints Eric Davidson as Chief Commercial Officer. Davidson brings over 20 years of medical leadership experience.
news.summary: Mendaera Inc., a healthcare tech firm in San Mateo, California, has named Eric Davidson as its inaugural Chief Commercial Officer (CCO). The company is developing a unique handheld robotic platform aimed at transforming prevalent interventional procedures. Davidson, bringing over 20 years of medical leadership experience, will oversee commercial strategies for Mendaera as it gears up for the launch of its innovative robotic technologies in the market.

news.headline: Goodfood will report Q1 2025 results on Jan 21, 2025, and host virtual AGM same day. CEO, President, and CFO to review.
news.summary: Goodfood Market Corp., a prominent Canadian online meal solutions company, is set to disclose its Q1 Fiscal 2025 financial results on January 21, 2025, before market commencement. The CEO Jonathan Ferrari, President and COO Neil Cuggy, and CFO Roslane Aouameur will conduct a conference call at 8:00 a.m. (ET) to discuss the outcomes. Furthermore, the Annual General Meeting of Shareholders is scheduled for the same day at 10:00 a.m. (ET), being held virtually via the TSX's online meeting platform. Stay tuned for the latest updates on the company's performance.

news.headline: Seres Therapeutics reports positive results from SER-155 Phase 1b study in allo-HSCT recipients, showing improved epithelial barrier integrity. Live biotherapeutics show promise in targeting various diseases.
news.summary: In a recent Phase 1b study, biomarker data for SER-155 in recipients of allogeneic hematopoietic stem cell transplantation (allo-HSCT) revealed promising results. The study showed that SER-155 effectively enhanced epithelial barrier integrity and reduced systemic inflammatory biomarkers when compared to a placebo. These findings underscore the potential of Seres' live biotherapeutics in addressing inflammatory and immune-related conditions. This data suggests that SER-155 has the capacity to deliver positive impacts, offering new hope for individuals affected by such conditions.

news.headline: Zown grows by 400% in 2023, introduces new products and partnerships to help Canadians buy and sell homes.
news.summary: A real estate brokerage company, Zown, based in Toronto, is revolutionizing the home buying and selling process in Canada with its advanced technology. They have experienced a remarkable 400% growth since transitioning into a brokerage in 2023. In response to the increasing need for tailored assistance and guidance in the homeownership process, Zown has launched a range of new products and partnerships. These initiatives are aimed at making homeownership easier and more cost-effective for Canadians. Through these innovations, Zown is striving to meet the demand for personalized support and education in navigating the complexities of buying and selling homes.

news.headline: GXO establishes collaboration with Calliope in Italy, expanding its operations in the country.
Fonon Technologies will attend NCMS Technology Showcase at Pearl Harbor Naval Shipyard from January 21 to 23.
news.summary: Fonon Technologies, a company specializing in laser technologies for material processing and defense applications, will be participating in the NCMS Technology Showcase at the Pearl Harbor Naval Shipyard & Intermediate Maintenance Facility. The event is scheduled to run from January 21 to 23 and is organized by PHNSY & IMF in collaboration with the National Center for Manufacturing Sciences. Fonon Technologies will showcase its cutting-edge laser systems during the event, which serves as a platform for companies to demonstrate their innovations to a wide audience. This year's showcase is expected to draw significant attention with attendance projections reaching high numbers.

news.headline: Boralex and Walpole Island FN secure $172M for 80MW/320MWh Tilbury Battery Storage project in Ontario.
news.summary: In Montreal on January 9, 2025, Boralex Inc. and Walpole Island First Nation finalized a $172 million financing deal for the Tilbury Battery Storage project in Lakeshore, Ontario. This project, with a capacity of 80 MW / 320 MWh, marks a significant collaboration between the two entities.

news.headline: Laser Photonics Corp (LPC), a global leader in industrial laser systems, to deliver repeat CleanTech system to Acuren.
news.summary: Laser Photonics Corporation (NASDAQ: LASE), a prominent developer of industrial laser systems, has recently fulfilled a substantial order from Acuren, a renowned asset protection services provider. John Armstrong, Executive Vice President of LPC, expressed excitement about Acuren's return as a customer. Acuren specializes in engineering consulting and nondestructive testing (NDT). This latest order signifies a strong partnership between the two companies.

news.headline: 72% of women are skipping OB/GYN exams for cervical cancer screenings, seeking more convenience and less discomfort in testing options.
news.summary: A recent survey conducted by BD (Becton, Dickinson and Company) disclosed that 72% of women are actively seeking more convenient and less discomforting testing options. The survey results were made public in Franklin Lakes, N.J. on January 9, 2025. BD, a prominent medical technology company, is recognized on the NYSE as BDX.

news.headline: Brunswick Exploration reports drilling 37 meters at 1.14% at Mirage Project in Quebec. Dyke extension intersected mineralized intervals.
news.summary: Montreal, January 9, 2025 - Brunswick Exploration Inc. is excited to announce the latest findings from the Mirage summer drilling initiative in the Eeyou Istchee-James Bay region of Quebec, near the Trans-Taiga Road. This project concentrated on the Central Zone, particularly the MR-6 and MR-3 dykes as highlighted in the October 8, 2024 press release. The drilling campaign successfully revealed substantial and mineral-rich sections both along the extensions and at deeper levels. Brunswick Exploration Inc. is listed as BRW on the TSX-V, BRWXF on the OTCQB, and 1XQ on the Frankfurt Stock Exchange.

news.headline: Endeavour Silver produced 4.5M oz Silver, 39K oz Gold (7.6M Silver Eq Oz) in 2024; Q4 brought 824K oz Silver, 9K oz Gold.
news.summary: In Vancouver, Canada, on January 9, 2025, Endeavour Silver Corp. announced its production figures for the full year of 2024. The company disclosed that they produced 4,471,824 ounces of silver and 39,047 ounces of gold, resulting in a total of 7.6 million equivalent ounces of silver. For the fourth quarter alone, they reported 824,529 ounces of silver, 9,075 ounces of gold, and a total of 1.6 million equivalent ounces of silver. Endeavour Silver Corp. is listed on the NYSE under the ticker symbol EXK and on the TSX as EDR.

news.headline: Poll: Canadian Dollar forecasted to gain 3% to 1.3950/USD in 12 months (vs 1.4020 in December poll)
Poll: Canadian Dollar forecasted to edge 0.5% higher to 1.43/USD in 3 months (vs 1.4034 in December poll)
IGT is first gaming supplier to achieve iCAP Ready Accreditation from NCPG for responsible gaming standards.
news.summary: IGT has been granted a new accreditation for maintaining top-notch responsible gaming standards in its iLottery operations. The accreditation, specific to the supplier, marks IGT as the first gaming industry supplier to receive such recognition. The announcement was made on January 9, 2025, in London. International Game Technology PLC, trading under the symbol NYSE: IGT, has shown a commitment to responsible gaming practices.

news.headline: Global light vehicle sales to reach 85.1 million units in 2025, up by 1.3% from 2024, as reported by MarketsandMarkets.
news.summary: In Delray Beach, Florida, a recent report by MarketsandMarkets revealed that global light vehicle sales reached 84.0 million units in 2024. Projections indicate a slight increase to 85.1 million units in 2025, representing a year-over-year growth of 1.3%. This growth in light vehicle sales highlights a positive trend in the automotive industry.

news.headline: Brookfield Asset Management renews bid to buy up to 37,123,295 shares, about 10% of its floating shares, until Jan 12, 2026.
news.summary: On January 9, 2025, Brookfield Asset Management Ltd. (NYSE: BAM, TSX: BAM) announced approval for the renewal of its normal course issuer bid, allowing the purchase of up to 37,123,295 Class A Limited Voting Shares, equivalent to approximately 10% of the company's outstanding Class A Shares public float. The purchases will occur on the NYSE, TSX, and/or alternative trading systems from January 13, 2025, to January 12, 2026. Brookfield Asset Management will acquire the Class A Shares at market price during this period, or at a permissible alternative price.

news.headline: Helen of Troy Limited to participate in ICR Conference 2025 in Orlando, Florida on Jan 13, 2025. CEO, CFO, and SVP attending.
news.summary: Helen of Troy Limited, known for its consumer home, outdoor, beauty, and wellness products, (NASDAQ: HELE) disclosed its involvement in investor gatherings and a fireside chat session at the ICR Conference 2025 in Grande Lakes Orlando, Florida on January 13, 2025. Key executives, including Chief Executive Officer Noel Geoffroy, Chief Financial Officer Brian Grass, and Senior Vice President Sabrina McKee, will be part of the event to discuss company strategies and financial insights.

news.headline: Cameco acknowledges Westinghouse President and CEO Patrick Fragman's resignation effective March 31, 2025.
news.summary: In Saskatoon, Saskatchewan, Cameco (TSX: CCO; NYSE: CCJ) recognizes the significant contributions of Patrick Fragman as President and CEO of Westinghouse Electric Company. He has announced his decision to step down from his role at Westinghouse, effective March 31, 2025, to prioritize spending time with his family in Europe. Patrick has been at the helm of Westinghouse since 2019. Westinghouse, a prominent figure in the nuclear energy sector, is known for its innovative practices and leadership.

news.headline: First U.S. crypto tax evasion case results in a two-year prison sentence and $1M restitution for underreporting $4M Bitcoin gains. IRS sets precedent. Rogue crypto traders beware!
news.summary: The IRS and DOJ recently secured the first criminal tax evasion conviction focused on cryptocurrency, sentencing Frank Richard Ahlgren III to two years in prison and over one million dollars in restitution for underreporting gains from selling four million dollars worth of Bitcoin. Another case involving Roger Keith Ver, known as "Bitcoin Jesus," faces an eight-count federal indictment for tax evasion and other charges related to not paying "exit tax" on over one hundred thirty-one thousand Bitcoins. Ver could face up to 20 years per mail fraud count and significant prison time if convicted. These cases serve as a warning to taxpayers in the cryptocurrency arena to comply with tax laws, as the government is cracking down on crypto tax evasion. The IRS has expanded its investigation reach beyond U.S. borders with John Doe summonses and high-risk letters.

news.headline: Carter funeral unites political rivals at solemn event.
